title: Oyo govt Pays N60m To Pensioners Monthly - FinancialEDGE
description: The Oyo State Government has disclosed that the sum of Sixty Million Naira (N60m) is being paid to state Pensioners monthly in a bid to clear their outstanding arrears. html:
The Oyo State Government has disclosed that the sum of Sixty Million Naira (N60m) is being paid to state Pensioners monthly in a bid to clear their outstanding arrears.

The Chairman, Oyo State Pension Board, Hon.Tunji Adekunle, stated this at a press briefing in his office on Wednesday.

He also confirmed that a total number of Twenty-two Thousand, Two Hundred and twelve (22, 212) pensioners are currently on the payroll of Oyo State Pension Board as of April, 2025.

He emphasized that Governor Seyi Makinde’s administration remains deeply committed to the welfare of pensioners, stressing that since the administration’s inception, pensions have been consistently disbursed on or before the 25th of each month, alongside timely payment of salaries for active workers.

He further explained that new pensioners are being added to the payroll on a monthly basis.

“Since the beginning of this year alone, the State Pension Board has enrolled 142 pensioners in January, 175 in February, 171 in March, and 143 in April”, he said.

In his remarks, the Permanent Secretary, Mr. Hammed Bello commended Governor Seyi Makinde for his unwavering support for retirees.

He stated that the timely and consistent payment of pensions has brought relief to many pensioners and strengthened their trust with the State Government.

He reiterated the Board’s dedication to transparency, accountability, and continuous improvements in pension administration across the state.
